rationale: Heavy frictions across the board: a dark 3D crime-scene render (navy/gold/brown palette, photoreal forensics) has zero visual or thematic continuity with a flat 2D green-felt solitaire game, and the hook telegraphs a narrative/mystery game (Merge Mansion, June's Journey) not a card sorter. Mechanic bridge is nearly impossible — there's no plausible 2-second pivot from "police tape on a mailbox" into draw/stack/foundation gameplay without feeling bait-and-switch, which risks store-policy flags and tanks post-install retention. Only weak positive is loose audience overlap (adult casual players enjoy mystery tropes); do not greenlight this archetype for Voodoo Solitaire — if curiosity is the desired pitch, commission a card-native mystery hook (e.g., "solve the case by clearing the tableau") instead.
